stem: A factory produces PCB boards using two sources. SMT Line 1 handles 51% of production with a known defect rate of 3.0%. SMT Line 2 handles 49% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 7.8% and 11.8%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected PCB board is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from SMT Line 2?

ground_truth.answer: P(SMT Line 2 | defective) ranges from 0.714 to 0.791 as SMT Line 2's defect rate varies from 7.8% to 11.8%. Point estimate at 9.8%: 0.758.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.714, 0.791] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
